top_signals.title: Intel Surges on Apple Deal | Open Interest 6/18/2026
top_signals.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Trump’s Iran deal reopens Hormuz. We talk to Livia Paggi at J.S. Held about the questions that remain. Plus,  Kevin Warsh shocks markets at the Fed, Intel rides the made-in-America AI chip push, and LIV Golf’s Scott O’Neil fights for the league’s future. Plus, we are live from the Knicks parade in New York City as festivities kick off. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Get Relief Rally as US-Iran Deal Sinks Oil: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Wall Street staged a comeback that saw stocks climbing as oil dropped after a peace deal between the US and Iran spurred optimism that a revival of the Strait of Hormuz will ease inflation risks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Germany moves two ships to Djibouti, eyeing multinational Hormuz mission this summer
top_signals.summary: Germany is among a number of countries that have offered help in securing the shipping lane, but only if an Iran ceasefire deal turns out to be lasting.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Air Force awards first CCA production contracts to General Atomics, Anduril
top_signals.summary: The U.S. Air Force has cleared its first Collaborative Combat Aircraft to enter production.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Azerbaijan Moves Into Uzbekistan’s Fast-Growing Mining Sector
top_signals.summary: Azerbaijan and Uzbekistan are strengthening economic connections, signing a variety of agreements covering mining, banking and tourism. Uzbek Prime Minister Abdulla Aripov and his Azerbaijani counterpart, Ali Asadov, presided over a signing ceremony on June 16 in Tashkent of a cooperation agreement under which the two countries will jointly develop an Uzbek gold deposit as well as other mineral resources.  Ural Yusupov, Uzbekistan’s deputy mining minister, previously indicated that Tashkent was intent on conducting…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
